title: arthrostylidium queko
intro: Arthrostylidium queko, also known as the Quekó palm, is a species of flowering plant in the palm family (Arecaceae) native to the mountainous regions of the Andes in South America. The Quekó palm has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are believed to help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Arthrostylidium queko include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
